title: Specificity Showdown
description: Test your understanding of CSS specificity rules: which selector wins when multiple rules target the same element?
referenceSolutions: ID selectors have higher specificity than class selectors

validationRules.message: Select the correct answer about CSS specificity

hints.text: Specificity is calculated based on the types of selectors used. IDs are more specific than classes, which are more specific than elements.
The specificity hierarchy (low to high): element selectors, class selectors, ID selectors, inline styles. !important overrides all.
An ID selector (#nav) has higher specificity (0,1,0,0) than a class selector (.nav) which is (0,0,1,0), which beats an element selector (div) at (0,0,0,1).
